creator: pluknet@gmail.com
text:
I've got NMI on an almost idle system - FreeBSD 7.2-R amd64.  Whereas
the second box running the identical setup with identical h/w, and OS
version is ok.

It happened the second time for two last days.

I guess the reason may be in (hardware?) binary garbage seen once in a
while on serial port (loader, then ttyd0, even ddb).

I see in dmesg some time before panic:
<118>Feb  2 20:15:17 host init: getty repeating too quickly on port /dev/ttyd0, sleeping 30 secs
<118>Feb  2 20:15:47 host init: getty repeating too quickly on port /dev/ttyd0, sleeping 30 secs

I guess there's too much interrupts on sio0 comparing with others:

Fix: 

Not known.
How-To-Repeat: It's hardly reproducible.

I guess the reason may be in (hardware?) binary garbage
seen once in a while on serial port (loader, then ttyd0, even ddb).

My mileage varies: it can panic once per day, or I need to wait ~ for a month.
